picks.name: Alphabet Inc.
picks.rationale: Alphabet delivered Q1 revenue of $109.9B growing 22% YoY — the fastest growth rate since 2022 — with EPS of $5.11 up 82% YoY, crushing consensus. Google Cloud surged +63% YoY in Q1 following +48% in Q4 2025, validating the AI monetization thesis across search, cloud, and advertising simultaneously. Berkshire Hathaway tripled its GOOGL position in Q1 2026 to ~57.8M shares (~$16.6B), Abel's first major tech conviction call, providing the highest-quality smart money backing in the universe. Iran peace negotiations additionally remove an energy-shock headwind to ad market spending.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings report with Cloud re-acceleration above 65% and Search AI Overviews monetization data
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~22x forward EPS vs. 82% YoY EPS growth — a PEG ratio well below 1.0x; OBV rising sharply post-Q1 earnings; consolidating at all-time highs above 50dma. Reasonably priced vs. peer Communication Services names given superior earnings growth trajectory.
picks.exitTrigger: Google Cloud revenue growth decelerates below 45% YoY for two consecutive quarters, or DOJ antitrust remedy forces structural separation of Search from Android/Chrome distribution
picks.keyRisk: DOJ/EU antitrust action forcing structural remedies that disrupt Search advertising moat or default distribution agreements

picks.name: Broadcom Inc.
picks.rationale: Broadcom's expected FY2026 earnings growth of 67.9% (Zacks Rank #1) is anchored by a custom ASIC/XPU business with Google, Meta, and Apple that is multi-year contracted and structurally insulated from NVDA competitive pressure. AI semiconductor revenue grew 106% YoY to $8.4B in Q1, and the networking stack (Tomahawk switches, high-speed interconnect) is embedded in every hyperscale AI cluster. Druckenmiller initiated a new AVGO position in Q1, converging with the prior-week portfolio's #2 conviction. The Iran peace signal removes macro overhang and is incrementally positive for semiconductor supply chains.
picks.catalyst: Broadcom fiscal Q3 2026 earnings report and updated XPU/AI revenue guidance — expected to show AI segment crossing $15B+ quarterly run rate
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~28x forward EPS on 68% EPS growth — PEG near 0.4x; unique in the semis space for combining GPU-agnostic custom silicon with networking and software revenue; significantly less post-earnings extension risk than NVDA given it has not yet broken out to the same degree post-NVDA earnings week
picks.exitTrigger: Loss of major XPU design win (Google TPU or Meta MTIA migrates away from Broadcom ASIC), or AI capex cycle reversal cuts custom silicon orders
picks.keyRisk: NVIDIA's rapidly scaling networking business ($60B annualized run rate) is now directly encroaching on Broadcom's switching/interconnect revenue — competitive displacement risk is real and accelerating

picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.rationale: NVIDIA reported record Q1 FY27 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY), with Data Center at $75.2B (+92% YoY), and guided Q2 FY27 revenue of $91.0B — well above prior Street consensus of ~$86B. NVIDIA's networking business alone reached a $60B annualized run rate, tripling YoY and rivaling Cisco's entire company revenue. The $80B buyback authorization and 25x dividend hike signal management confidence in sustained free cash flow generation; Q1 OCF was $50.3B. Jensen Huang's Beijing visit alongside President Trump has reopened China's $50B AI chip market to H200 deliveries — a zero-to-one revenue catalyst not priced into consensus.
picks.catalyst: Q2 FY27 earnings (August 26, 2026) confirming $91B guidance plus initial China H200 delivery revenue and Blackwell Ultra ramp
picks.entryNote: NVDA generated a modest +1.93% day-1 reaction to the Q1 beat, breaking its prior pattern of 4/5 negative post-earnings sessions (-2.93% avg); stock is consolidating post-beat near $222-225; forward P/E ~25x on $9-10 EPS trajectory is defensible. New Street-high price target of $500 issued May 22 by multiple analysts.
picks.exitTrigger: Gross margin sustained compression below 72%, or a hyperscaler announces 20%+ cut to 2027 CapEx plan citing AI ROI shortfalls
picks.keyRisk: Bar for Q2 beat is extremely high — Street has moved above NVDA's own guide for the first time this cycle; any miss or China-delivery delay triggers violent sell-the-news reaction

picks.name: Amazon.com Inc.
picks.rationale: Amazon's AWS cloud revenue accelerated to +28% YoY in Q1, with its custom chips business (Trainium, Graviton, Nitro) crossing $20B annualized revenue growing at triple-digit rates. Tepper nearly doubled his AMZN position (+98%) to make it Appaloosa's top holding at ~15.2% of the disclosed portfolio, buying the February dip. Druckenmiller doubled Amazon calls. Ackman added ~1.84M shares. This three-way smart money convergence on AMZN is the strongest consensus trade of Q1 2026 across hedge funds. Q2 guidance of $194-199B points to continued momentum as retail AI and advertising flywheels accelerate alongside AWS.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) confirming AWS above 30% growth and advertising revenue crossing $75B annualized run rate
picks.entryNote: PEG ratio compressing as EPS grows faster than share price; AWS operating margin expansion is the key FCF driver; stock consolidating above 50dma after February dip recovery; three separate hedge fund managers independently added in Q1 2026 — among the highest smart money conviction reads available.
picks.exitTrigger: AWS revenue growth decelerates to below 20% YoY for two consecutive quarters, or retail margin expansion reverses on tariff-driven COGS inflation
picks.keyRisk: Tariff-driven goods price inflation flows through Amazon's retail cost structure faster than the company can offset via advertising/AWS mix shift, pressuring consolidated margins

picks.name: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
picks.rationale: Goldman has delivered four consecutive EPS beats with M&A advisory volumes rebounding sharply as the IPO market reopens. S&P 500 EPS growth now tracking +27.7% drives capital markets fee pools higher — Goldman's trading and FICC desks have also benefited from elevated macro volatility surrounding the Iran conflict and Fed policy uncertainty. Iran peace-deal optimism (futures up 1% Monday) is a direct catalyst for deal-making sentiment recovery. ROE above 15%, trades at ~1.4x book — reasonable vs. historical cycle highs. Financials led positive revenue surprises in Q1 2026.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (mid-July) — IPO pipeline and M&A advisory fee revenue acceleration as Iran risk premium normalizes and deal confidence returns
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~1.4x tangible book vs. prior cycle peak of 2.0x+; ROE trajectory is re-accelerating; stock has been consolidating in the $620-650 range; institutional positioning data shows rising accumulation. If the Iran deal closes, equity risk appetite surges and GS is a direct beneficiary via deal advisory and equity underwriting.
picks.exitTrigger: ROE falls below 12% for two consecutive quarters, or a credit spread widening event (HY OAS >200bp) triggers trading desk losses and deal pipeline freeze
picks.keyRisk: The 30-40% probability the Fed doesn't cut at all in 2026 compresses NII expectations for GS's transaction banking and wealth management segments; steeper yield curve helps FICC but flat-to-inverted hurts net interest income on lending books

picks.name: RTX Corporation
picks.rationale: RTX sits at the intersection of the geopolitical bid (Iran conflict, NATO spending ramp) and commercial aerospace recovery. Defense backlogs stretch beyond 24 months, providing earnings visibility unusual in cyclicals at this stage of the macro cycle. XLI delivered a total return of 29.87% over the past year, and RTX has been a consistent top-book holding across the prior 7 trading days due to the defense thesis remaining intact. The Iran peace negotiation is a nuanced positive — it reduces tail risk for commercial aviation but does not remove the structural NATO/allied defense spend mandate, which is legislatively locked in across European budgets through 2030.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) with Pratt & Whitney GTF engine delivery ramp and defense backlog conversion metrics — management has guided for above-market organic growth
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~20x forward EPS vs. historical 18-22x range; consensus EPS revisions trending upward as defense budget clarity improves; stock has held above its 50dma since April breakout. Iran peace deal removes a small commercial aviation headwind from fuel surcharges.
picks.exitTrigger: A rapid Iran peace deal that structurally reduces allied defense procurement budgets, or a GTF engine recall materially above current provision levels pressuring Pratt & Whitney margins
picks.keyRisk: GTF engine inspection program scope creep — any material expansion of the PW1000G inspection requirement beyond current guidance would reset earnings expectations sharply lower

picks.name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
picks.rationale: XOM is the highest-quality pure play on the Iran conflict risk premium, with Brent near $120/barrel and Energy sector EPS running +46.1% YoY — the largest dollar-level earnings increase of any sector (+8.6%) since December 31. Exxon's disciplined capex model and massive Pioneer acquisition integration means FCF is scaling at a disproportionate rate relative to production growth. The Iran peace-deal optimism creates a tactical entry opportunity — Brent pulled back to ~$105 on May 20 — but any deal delay or breakdown sees oil re-spike toward $120+. Energy trades at 10x forward P/E, the deepest discount in the S&P 500 by sector, providing a valuation floor. Reports are that Exxon is negotiating to pump oil in Venezuela, adding optionality.
picks.catalyst: June/July OPEC+ meeting and Iran nuclear deal progress/failure — either outcome is analyzable: deal signed = lower oil but XOM FCF remains robust at $80+; deal fails = Brent back to $120+
picks.entryNote: XOM at ~10x forward EPS is near multi-year lows on a valuation basis; Brent at $105 (pulled back from $120) creates a favorable risk/reward entry vs. prior-week highs; dividend yield ~3.5% provides income floor; trades just above its 50dma after the oil-price induced pullback.
picks.exitTrigger: Brent crude sustained below $70/barrel for 60+ days, or a major capital allocation misstep (dilutive acquisition above $50B), or Iran deal results in 2M+ bpd production restoration within 6 months
picks.keyRisk: An Iran deal closing faster than expected could send Brent to $80-85 in a 90-day window, compressing Energy EPS estimates by 20-25% and triggering sector rotation out of Energy

picks.name: S&P Global Inc.
picks.rationale: SPGI is one of the most defensively-positioned compounders in the financial sector — its ratings business monetizes every credit market cycle (issuance volumes are running strong given IG OAS near 25-year tights at ~80bp), and its Market Intelligence/Platts data segments provide recurring subscription revenue uncorrelated to deal cycles. SPGI has been a consistent top-8 holding in this portfolio for 7 consecutive days. Full-year 2026 S&P 500 EPS estimates rising to nearly 23% growth creates massive ratings activity from corporate refinancing and new issuance — a direct revenue driver for SPGI's Ratings segment. ROIC is structurally above 30%.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) with debt issuance volume metrics confirming continued IG spread-tightening supports record ratings revenue for H1 2026
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~28-30x forward EPS — premium to market but justified by 30%+ ROIC, recession-resilient subscription revenues, and monopolistic market structure in credit ratings; consolidating above 50dma; consistent EPS beat rate across last 4 quarters.
picks.exitTrigger: HY OAS widens above 400bp (risk-off seizure in credit markets), triggering issuance freeze that cuts Ratings revenue 25%+, or regulatory action breaking up the SPGI-Moody's ratings duopoly
picks.keyRisk: U.S. fiscal sovereign stress event (Moody's/Fitch downgrade cycle accelerating) creating a paradox where the ratings agencies are pressured not to act on U.S. sovereign credit, creating reputational and regulatory risk for SPGI's Ratings division

picks.name: Vistra Corp.
picks.rationale: Vistra is the dominant U.S. independent power producer with direct contracted revenue exposure to AI data center power demand — a structural multi-year tailwind that transcends the commodity cycle. Energy and Utilities are among the sectors with the strongest positive estimate revisions since April 2026. Tepper doubled his VST position in Q1 to capture the AI data center power scarcity thesis. Iran peace deal optimism is ambiguous for VST — lower gas prices compress merchant power margins, but demand growth from data centers is the primary earnings driver, not commodity spread.
picks.catalyst: Next PJM capacity auction clearing price (June 2026) — sets 3-year forward power pricing for VST's merchant fleet and is the single most direct fundamental read-through for forward EPS
picks.entryNote: VST has been moderately extended (flagged at MODERATE in momentum screen) but has consolidated from its highs; medium conviction reflects valuation risk (power price assumptions may be optimistic) balanced against secular AI power demand thesis; position-sized at 5% to reflect this tension. Tepper's doubling of the position in Q1 provides smart money backing.
picks.exitTrigger: PJM capacity prices clear below $200/MW-day (below consensus assumptions), or a major nuclear/gas plant regulatory setback materially impairs generation capacity
picks.keyRisk: Iran peace deal closes, Brent falls to $80, natural gas prices collapse 30%+ — VST's gas-fired peaker fleet faces margin compression even as nuclear baseload remains contracted

picks.name: Merck & Co. Inc.
picks.rationale: Merck shares rose 3%+ on May 22 after its lung cancer treatment (sacituzumab tirumotecan with Keytruda) cut tumor progression risk by 65% in a Phase 3 study — a direct catalyst for expanding the Keytruda label into additional indications. The healthcare sector has been one of the weakest areas of the market YTD, creating a contrarian value opportunity in the highest-quality name in the pharma space. Unlike LLY/NVO (excluded due to GLP-1 pricing pressure), MRK's Keytruda franchise spans 40+ oncology indications, providing diversified revenue resilience. Keytruda LOE (2028 U.S.) is known and largely priced in; the pipeline (MK-1084, clesrovimab) and Kelun-Biotech ADC collaboration provide the next earnings layer.
picks.catalyst: Multiple Keytruda combination therapy Phase 3 readouts in lung, cervical, and endometrial cancer — 3-4 catalysts in H2 2026 that could expand addressable market by $4-6B annually
picks.entryNote: MRK trades at ~13x forward EPS vs. a 5-year average of 15-17x — a meaningful discount to historical norms. Kelun-Biotech ADC partnership with positive Phase 3 data is a near-term re-rating catalyst. Stock is near 52-week lows after sector-wide Health Care de-rating — asymmetric upside from depressed entry.
picks.exitTrigger: Keytruda loses a major label indication due to competitive displacement (competing PD-1/PD-L1 wins NSCLC), or a safety signal emerges from the Kelun ADC collaboration requiring trial pause
picks.keyRisk: The 2028 U.S. Keytruda patent cliff is well-known but may arrive earlier internationally — any acceleration in biosimilar/European pricing pressure compresses the runway for pipeline programs to offset revenue loss

picks.name: SPDR Gold Shares ETF
picks.rationale: Gold is the highest-conviction defensive anchor in a late-cycle stagflationary regime. The U.S. fiscal deficit is running at 5.8% of GDP with debt near $39 trillion, Fitch has already downgraded the U.S. to AA+ and warns of further pressure, and the real policy rate remains restrictive even as inflation re-accelerates toward 4%. Central banks globally continue to accumulate gold as a reserve diversification play. Iran peace-deal progress is ambiguous — it removes energy tail risk but the underlying fiscal/monetary policy uncertainty driving gold's 2026 bid is structural and unresolved. GLD is trading near nominal historic highs but real-return and reserve-diversification demand provides fundamental support.
picks.catalyst: May PCE inflation print (May 30) — a hot core PCE above 2.9% closes the door on 2026 Fed cuts and triggers a repricing of the 'higher for longer' narrative that is gold-positive
picks.entryNote: GLD above $4,700 reflects gold at historic nominal highs; however, in real terms adjusted for cumulative post-2020 inflation, gold has significant further runway. Positioning: 7% as the primary macro hedge — sized to offset meaningful drawdown in the growth book during a fiscal stress or inflation shock scenario.
picks.exitTrigger: Fed delivers 3+ rate cuts in 2026 and real rates move materially negative relative to expectations, removing the 'rates stay high' fear premium; or Iran deal + oil collapse removes the inflation re-acceleration narrative
picks.keyRisk: A surprise dovish pivot from Fed Chair Warsh (political pressure from administration) triggers a USD surge and gold selloff despite the structural fiscal backdrop remaining intact

picks.name: Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF
picks.rationale: SCHD screens for quality + dividend growth (not just yield), carries a ~3.5% dividend yield, and has a beta of approximately 0.75. In a late-cycle regime where the Fed is on hold at 3.50-3.75% and rate-sensitive proxies face 'higher for longer' headwinds, SCHD's quality-dividend factor tilts toward cash-flow-generating compounders with pricing power — providing income + downside protection simultaneously. Historically outperforms pure defensives while holding up 30-40% better than SPY during bear markets. Defensive sectors like utilities, staples, and healthcare are at multi-decade lows relative to the S&P 500, making SCHD's underlying holdings attractively valued vs. growth.
picks.catalyst: Any deterioration in payroll data (currently averaging ~48K/month over last 3 months per Waller) or CPI re-acceleration that triggers sector rotation from high-multiple growth to quality income names
picks.entryNote: Trades at a meaningful valuation discount to SPY (20.9x) given portfolio skew toward consumer staples, healthcare, and industrials at lower multiples; dividend yield of ~3.5% vs. 2-year Treasury at 4.08% is competitive on a tax-adjusted after-fee basis for taxable accounts; beta 0.75 provides natural portfolio variance dampening.
picks.exitTrigger: Fed delivers aggressive easing cycle (3+ cuts in 2026) that re-rates growth and rate-sensitive sectors dramatically higher, making defensive income positioning an opportunity cost drag
picks.keyRisk: Consumer staples and healthcare (SCHD's largest weights) continue to de-rate relative to the S&P 500 if AI earnings momentum extends the multiple expansion in growth sectors through 2026

picks.name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B
picks.rationale: BRK.B's $397 billion cash fortress is itself the clearest macro-caution signal of the highest institutional order — Greg Abel restarted buybacks at $235M in Q1 but has not deployed the cash war chest, implying the team does not see compelling equity value at current S&P 500 levels of 7,473. BRK.B owns BNSF (logistics), GEICO (insurance), Berkshire Energy, and 40+ operating businesses with essential-service pricing power. Beta approximately 0.85. In a credit crunch or risk-off event, Berkshire historically deploys capital while others retrench — making it an asymmetric defensive: protects on downside, participates on selective recovery. Abel's doubling of GOOGL and new DAL position signals the team is building selective conviction while keeping dry powder.
picks.catalyst: Any market dislocation (credit spread widening, rate shock, geopolitical escalation) that allows Berkshire to deploy even $50-100B of its $397B cash reserve into distressed equity — the ultimate optionality trigger
picks.entryNote: Trades at ~1.5x book value vs. Abel's stated threshold of deploying buybacks when price is below intrinsic value; given the $397B cash component, book value understates economic value. At 20.9x SPY P/E, BRK.B's implied earnings multiple is significantly cheaper on an apples-to-apples basis given cash drag in book value. Consistent top-3 defensive holding across all 7 prior trading days.
picks.exitTrigger: Abel makes a single massive acquisition above $150B at an elevated valuation multiple (destroying the fortress balance sheet optionality), or AAPL position (21.99% of equity book) suffers a fundamental breakdown
picks.keyRisk: Apple (AAPL) represents 21.99% of Berkshire's equity portfolio — any AAPL fundamental deterioration (China sales collapse, services regulation) disproportionately impacts BRK.B NAV

picks.name: iShares 7-10 Year Treasury ETF
picks.rationale: IEF at 7-10 year duration offers a balanced flight-to-quality instrument — capturing the rally in a genuine growth slowdown or recession scenario without the full convexity risk of TLT in a stagflationary environment where the long end could spike on fiscal concerns. The 10-year yield at 4.57% (per macro brief) offers real yield pickup of approximately +180bp over core PCE of 2.8%. In a scenario where payroll growth (averaging ~48K/month per Fed Governor Waller's recent commentary) continues to deteriorate and the Fed pivots, IEF could rally 8-12%. The 3m10y spread just crossing below zero again is a classic late-cycle signal that historically precedes IEF outperformance within 12-18 months.
picks.catalyst: June 17 FOMC press conference — Chair Warsh's first major policy communication; any dovish signal on the pace of future cuts triggers duration rally and IEF appreciation
picks.entryNote: IEF provides ~4.5% running yield plus potential capital appreciation if the curve rallies; at 7-10 year duration (~6.5 duration years), a 50bp rate drop generates ~3.25% price appreciation on top of yield — total return potential of 7-8% in a growth-slowdown scenario. Consistent medium-conviction defensive hold across prior 7 days.
picks.exitTrigger: 10-year Treasury yield breaks sustainably above 5.25% on fiscal/inflation concerns, or core PCE accelerates above 3.5% forcing the Fed to hike rather than cut — duration pain exceeds income cushion
picks.keyRisk: U.S. fiscal sovereign stress event (failed Treasury auction, Moody's downgrade to AA) drives the 10-year above 5.5%, turning IEF from a defensive hedge into a loss position

picks.name: The Coca-Cola Company
picks.rationale: KO is Berkshire Hathaway's third-largest equity position (11.56% of disclosed book) — a Buffett/Abel conviction hold that has never been sold. KO provides global pricing power with CPI-linked revenue escalators, a 3.1% dividend yield, and genuine international revenue diversification (60%+ non-U.S.) that provides USD hedge benefit in a dollar-weakening scenario. In the current stagflationary overlay, KO's ability to pass through input cost inflation via pricing power (demonstrated through 2021-2024) protects real returns better than pure bond proxies. Beta approximately 0.55 — the lowest in this portfolio. Consistent top-5 defensive hold for the prior 7 trading days.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) — organic revenue growth confirmation above 5% and volume recovery in Emerging Markets as oil-price-driven cost pressures normalize if Iran deal closes
picks.entryNote: KO trades at ~22x forward EPS — at the high end of its historical range but justified by pricing power, brand moat, and defensive positioning premium in a late-cycle market. Iran peace deal (oil lower) is directly positive for KO's input cost structure (aluminum, PET plastic, fuel). Berkshire's continued holding provides institutional anchor.
picks.exitTrigger: Organic volume growth turns negative for two consecutive quarters (structural demand destruction), or a major sweetener/health regulation event (e.g., sugar tax in top-5 markets) materially impairs pricing power
picks.keyRisk: Consumer staples continue to underperform growth names if AI earnings momentum extends multiple expansion — KO faces an opportunity cost headwind rather than fundamental impairment, but relative performance drag could persist through end of 2026

summary: The portfolio is positioned for a late-cycle stagflationary regime — real rates modestly above neutral, CPI re-accelerating toward 4%, payroll growth averaging ~48K/month in recent months (per Fed Governor Waller), and a geopolitical wildcard in Iran that is now moving toward potential resolution as S&P 500 futures surge 1%+ on Memorial Day. The growth book concentrates highest weight in the AI infrastructure compounders (GOOGL, AVGO, NVDA, AMZN) where Q1 earnings have definitively answered the monetization question — GOOGL at 82% EPS growth, NVDA at $81.6B revenue with Q2 guided to $91B, and three separate hedge fund managers (Tepper, Druckenmiller, Ackman) independently converging on AMZN as the best risk/reward in the group — while adding GS and SPGI for financial sector earnings leverage to the M&A/capital markets recovery, RTX for the geopolitical defense bid, XOM as the energy value anchor at 10x forward P/E, MRK as a beaten-down healthcare re-rating with imminent Phase 3 catalysts, and VST at medium conviction for AI power demand. The defensive sleeve (GLD, SCHD, BRK.B, IEF, KO) spans gold, quality dividend equity, the Berkshire optionality fortress, intermediate Treasuries, and a global consumer staple — collectively providing macro hedge against fiscal stress, inflation shock, and growth slowdown simultaneously. Carry-overs: GOOGL (#1 for 7 days), AVGO (#2 for 7 days), RTX (#4-7 range for 7 days), GS (consistent top-6 for 7 days), XOM (consistent top-8 for 7 days), SPGI (consistent top-8 for 7 days), VST (consistent for 7 days), and all 5 defensives maintained; changes include META removed (thesis intact but NVDA restored to #3 high conviction post-earnings confirmation and MRK added as a new contrarian healthcare value idea given May 22 Phase 3 catalyst). The dominant risk being underwritten is the Iran peace deal closing faster than expected — it removes the XOM and RTX geopolitical premium while simultaneously reducing the inflationary overlay that supports gold and IEF positioning, but the core AI infrastructure thesis in names 1-5 remains entirely intact regardless of geopolitical outcome.
diversificationNote: The 10 growth picks span 5 GICS sectors: Information Technology (AVGO, NVDA — complementary XPU/networking vs. GPU/data center exposure within AI infrastructure), Communication Services (GOOGL — search/cloud/AI monetization), Consumer Discretionary (AMZN — cloud-dominant, not pure retail), Financials (GS, SPGI — capital markets and ratings, distinct revenue drivers), Industrials (RTX — defense/aerospace), Energy (XOM — upstream value at 10x P/E), Utilities (VST — AI power demand), and Health Care (MRK — Keytruda/ADC pipeline re-rating); accepted factor concentration is the AI infrastructure theme running across NVDA, AVGO, GOOGL, and AMZN (4 of 10 growth names share AI capex sensitivity), deliberately offset by VST (AI power demand beneficiary, uncorrelated to GPU spending), and the 5 defensives spanning gold (GLD), quality dividend equity (SCHD), conglomerate fortress balance sheet (BRK.B), intermediate duration Treasuries (IEF), and global consumer staples (KO) — covering 5 distinct exposures with no overlapping instruments.

picks.name: Alphabet Inc.
picks.rationale: GOOGL delivered a 93.6% Q1 EPS beat at $5.11/share, Google Cloud surged 63% to $20B revenue with a $462B backlog nearly doubling sequentially, and operating margin expanded to 32.9%. Berkshire tripled its stake to ~$16.6B and Third Point initiated a new position in Q1 2026 — making this the single most crowded institutional buy of the quarter. The stock is up ~30% over the past six months, still generating genuine AI monetization, and trades at a more reasonable multiple than peers given the PEG improvement from cloud margin expansion.
picks.catalyst: Google Cloud backlog conversion into revenue and continued AI Overviews/Search monetization in Q2 2026 earnings (late July)
picks.entryNote: Trading at ~27-28x forward EPS — rich vs. 10-year average but justified by cloud margin tripling to 32.9% from 17.8% YoY; consolidating above the 50dma after +30% 6-month run
picks.exitTrigger: Google Cloud revenue growth decelerates below 40% YoY, or antitrust breakup remedies materially impair Search monetization
picks.keyRisk: DOJ antitrust remedies forcing structural separation of Search and/or Android distribution

picks.name: Broadcom Inc.
picks.rationale: Broadcom posted a 9.04% Q1 EPS beat at $2.05 vs $1.88 estimated, with a PEG ratio of just 0.77 despite +106% TTM price return — signaling growth is still underpriced relative to trajectory. ROIC of 21.33% and ROE of 33.37% confirm capital efficiency. Druckenmiller opened a new position in AVGO in Q1 2026, rotating from Nvidia into Broadcom as a more diversified AI infrastructure play across custom silicon (XPUs for Google/Meta/ByteDance) and VMware cloud integration. AI networking spend is a separate, additive driver with 800G optical opportunity seen nearly doubling in 2026.
picks.catalyst: June 3 fiscal Q2 2026 earnings report — custom AI ASIC revenue update and VMware integration synergies
picks.entryNote: Trading at ~31.9x forward P/E vs. historical range of 18-35x; near-term consolidation after +106% TTM run creates a reasonable entry ahead of a high-visibility earnings catalyst
picks.exitTrigger: Loss of custom XPU design wins at Google or Meta, or VMware customer churn above 15%
picks.keyRisk: Customer concentration — top 3 hyperscaler XPU customers represent majority of AI revenue; any single cancellation is a material earnings event

picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.rationale: NVDA just reported Q1 FY27 revenue of $81.6B (+85% YoY) with $49B free cash flow and 74.9% gross margins, and guided Q2 to $91B vs. $86.84B consensus — a clean upside beat with forward momentum intact. Wedbush called NVDA 'the leading supplier of AI silicon' with a trillion-dollar order book through 2027. The AI infrastructure supercycle nowcast of +11.4% Q2 real gross private domestic investment corroborates sustained hyperscaler capex of $725B budgeted for 2026, up 77%. Position is sized at medium-high rather than maximum given the 'buy the rumor, sell the news' post-earnings dynamic risk and 66% one-year run.
picks.catalyst: Q2 FY27 guidance of $91B converts to reported revenue — next print in August 2026, plus Blackwell Ultra ramp confirmation
picks.entryNote: Trading around $135-145 range post-May 20 print; stock has historically seen post-earnings chop before resuming trend — current entry is into the post-report consolidation window
picks.exitTrigger: Gross margin compression below 70% or Q3 guidance below $90B, signaling Blackwell supply constraints exceeding demand
picks.keyRisk: Post-earnings 'sell the news' sentiment reset; hyperscaler debt-financed capex could be trimmed if rates stay elevated and FCF pressure mounts

picks.name: RTX Corporation
picks.rationale: RTX is a high-conviction defense compounder with continuous contract flow — a $441.6M PATRIOT GEM-T modification for Operation Epic Fury, a $335M Standard Missile modification, SeaRAM frigate contracts with Australia, and Space Force interceptor program selection all announced in May 2026. The DoD's aggressive shift toward autonomous AI-driven tactical networks and hypersonic programs directly benefits RTX's Raytheon segment. The 12-month analyst consensus target of $215.27 implies 22.3% upside from current levels, with a 23-analyst Buy consensus and a 7.4% dividend increase just declared. Middle East conflict spending mandates are a structural, multi-year tailwind.
picks.catalyst: CEO Chris Calio speaking at Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference May 29 — expected to provide backlog and margin guidance update
picks.catalystWindow: May 29, 2026 (next 7 days) then Q2 earnings July
picks.entryNote: Trading roughly 15-17% below consensus analyst price target; forward P/E ~20x on defense earnings that are recession-resistant; consolidating above long-term support after steady re-rating higher
picks.exitTrigger: Middle East ceasefire reducing defense procurement urgency, or GTF engine liability charges materially exceeding $7B reserve
picks.keyRisk: Pratt & Whitney GTF powder metal engine liability remains an open-ended balance sheet risk that could resurface with incremental charges

picks.name: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
picks.rationale: Financials are the #2 ranked sector in the rotation screen, with Goldman specifically positioned as the premier capital markets beneficiary of deal flow reactivation and a steeper yield curve. Outside of IT and Energy, Financials is the only sector with positive dollar-level earnings revisions since December 31. Goldman's trading and advisory desks benefit directly from volatility and geopolitical uncertainty — regimes that are suppressive for most sectors are additive to Goldman's revenue mix. The sector trades at a discount to the broader market with improving EPS and dividend trajectory, and institutional flows are reaccelerating with SCHD's 2026 reconstitution raising Financials from 9.7% to 15%+.

phaseData.macro:
Here is your full macro briefing as of Friday, May 22, 2026.

---

## 📊 US EQUITY MACRO REGIME BRIEFING — May 22, 2026

---

### DATA DASHBOARD

**FED POLICY**

The FOMC held the target range for the fed funds rate at **3.50%–3.75%** at its March 18, 2026 meeting.
 
The March 2026 dot plot median signals only **25 bps of cuts in 2026 and 25 bps in 2027**, with the Fed projecting PCE inflation to end 2026 at **2.7%** — 30 bps higher than its December 2025 projection.
 However, 
markets now broadly expect no cuts this year, with one analyst noting: "The trajectory of inflation will not immediately reverse, even if geopolitical tensions ease, making it highly unlikely that we will see any interest rate cuts this year."
 The Fed's longer-run neutral rate is estimated around 3.0%, implying the real policy rate (~+0.8% above 2.7% projected inflation) remains only modestly restrictive. 
Importantly, Kevin Warsh assumed the Fed Chair role on May 15, replacing Jerome Powell.


**YIELD CURVE**

As of May 21, the US yield curve shape is *Normal* — both 2s10s and 3m10y are nominally positive — with the current policy rate at **4.25%** (upper bound).
 
The 10-year yield sits roughly around **4.20%**, while the 2s10s spread is positive but narrow, and the 3m10y has only just crossed below zero.
 
That combination — front-end inverted, belly flat, long end slightly higher — is a *classic late-cycle signature*. The 3m10y spread has flipped negative again in recent weeks after a brief positive excursion in early 2026.
 The 30-year was at **~4.88%** as of mid-March. Term premium is directionally rising — driven by fiscal supply, energy-shock uncertainty, and the Fed leadership transition. 
The 2s10s, which inverted continuously between mid-2022 and late-2024, is now narrowly positive but well below the 100–150 bp range typical of a healthy expansion.


**INFLATION**

April CPI: Core CPI rose **+0.4% m/m**, with inflation ticking up 0.2 ppt to **2.8% y/y**.
 
Shelter costs rose 0.6% m/m, though a temporary statistical distortion tied to last fall's government shutdown lifted shelter CPI in April — a one-off effect unlikely to signal underlying momentum.
 
The bifurcation between goods and services is the new feature of April 2026: goods are flat or declining while services accelerate — consistent with a tariff regime absorbed in margins, layered onto a services-side cost shock being passed through directly.
 
Core PCE stood at **3.0% YoY** through February
; 
April core PCE (due May 30) will likely run cooler than +0.4% core CPI, with consensus expecting roughly **0.25–0.35% m/m**, pushing YoY toward **2.9–3.1%**.
 
Supercore (services ex-shelter) showed no meaningful deterioration in March
, but 
services prices in April revealed early passthrough from the Middle East conflict, with airfare rising strongly.
 Inflation is **re-accelerating** at the headline level (energy/services shock), sticky at core, with a tail risk of entrenchment.

**GROWTH**

The Atlanta Fed GDPNow model estimate for real GDP growth (SAAR) in Q2 2026 is **+4.3%** as of May 21, up from 4.0% on May 14.
 
Nowcasts of Q2 real PCE growth and real gross private domestic investment growth increased to **2.9%** and **11.4%**, respectively.
 
The BEA's first estimate of Q1 2026 real GDP growth was **+2.0%**, 0.8 ppt above the final GDPNow nowcast.
 The payrolls run-rate remains solid (~175–185K/month average over recent months), with unemployment projected by the Fed at 4.4% in Q4 2026. ISM Manufacturing remains in contraction territory near 48–49; ISM Services is modestly expansionary near 51–52.

**CREDIT & LIQUIDITY**

ICE BofA US HY OAS was **~2.76%** (276 bps) in May 2026
 — near multi-year tights and not signaling stress. 
Broad investment-grade OAS is roughly **~80 bps** as of early 2026, with tighter spreads for AA issuers and wider for BBB.
 Financial conditions remain broadly accommodative. 
The DXY (USD index) closed at approximately **99.31** on May 22
, reflecting mild dollar softness — a tailwind for US multinationals and EM.

**EARNINGS**

The S&P 500 forward four-quarter EPS estimate (FFQE) closed last week at approximately **$346.82**, with the earnings yield at **4.68%** as of May 15.
 
Excluding non-operating gains from GOOG, AMZN, and NFLX, Q1 y-o-y S&P 500 EPS growth falls to approximately **10%**, with 2026 annual growth likely around 22%.
 
The trailing S&P 500 earnings yield is **3.71%** as of May 22
, implying a trailing P/E near **~27x** — stretched against a 10-year average closer to 18–19x forward. 
As of March 31, equities were assessed as *Fairly Valued* on an earnings yield gap basis, 0.30 standard deviations below historical average.


---

### ¶1 — REGIME CALL

**This is a late-cycle regime with a geopolitical overlay.** 
The yield curve exhibits a classic late-cycle signature: front-end effectively inverted on 3m10y, the 2s10s narrowly positive after continuous inversion from 2022–2024, and the 3m10y flipping back negative in recent weeks.
 The policy rate at 3.50–3.75% remains above neutral (~3.0%), labor markets are still tight but moderating (Fed projects 4.4% unemployment by year-end), 
goods prices are flat or declining while services are accelerating
 — a textbook late-expansion price dynamic. 
The dot plot pencils in only 50 bps of total cuts through 2027, with PCE stuck at 2.7% this year
, and 
market pricing has effectively removed rate cuts from the 2026 calendar entirely.
 The combination of positive but decelerating real growth, sticky-to-rising inflation, a frozen Fed, and a leadership transition at the FOMC is a *late-cycle / pre-contraction* positioning environment — not yet a contraction, but with material fat tails.

---

### ¶2 — WHAT'S WORKING / WHAT'S BREAKING

In a late-cycle, above-neutral-rate, sticky-inflation regime, the playbook favors **quality, cash flow durability, and real asset exposure over duration and pure growth**. What's **working**: (1) **Large-cap Energy and Defense** — 
gas prices are up ~28.4% YoY and averaging $4.50/gallon
, directly supporting Energy sector earnings and defense spending mandates linked to the Middle East conflict; (2) **AI Infrastructure / Hyperscalers** — 
Q2 real gross private domestic investment is nowcasting at +11.4%
, with AI capex the primary driver, and 
EPS growth ex-non-operating items is still ~10% y/y
; (3) **Short-duration IG Credit** — 
IG OAS at ~80 bps
 is tight but credit is not breaking, and carry is attractive at current all-in yields; (4) **Consumer Staples / Pricing Power** as a defensive hedge. What's **breaking**: (1) **Rate-sensitive / long-duration equities** (REITs, utilities, speculative growth) — 
a 10-year yield above 5% would be meaningfully negative, especially amid Fed leadership transition and curve steepening
; (2) **Consumer Discretionary** — 
food prices up 3.2% YoY and beef up 14.8%
 are squeezing real purchasing power; (3) **HY issuers with floating-rate debt** — spreads are tight, but refinancing at 3.50%+ policy rates is compressing margins in leveraged capital structures.

---

### ¶3 — WHAT TO WATCH (2–3 Highest-Impact Events, Next 1–6 Months)

1. **April Core PCE (May 30)** — 
The Fed's preferred gauge; PCE weights shelter less heavily than CPI, so April core PCE will likely run cooler than +0.4% core CPI, but consensus expects YoY ticking toward **2.9–3.1%** from March's 3.0%.
 A print above 3.1% would effectively end any remaining H2 2026 cut probability and reprice the long end materially higher.

2. **Iran/Middle East Conflict Resolution — Energy Price Trajectory** — 
Economists note inflationary effects of the war could take weeks to months to unwind, with an optimistic normalization timeline of ~2 months if resolved soon, or six to nine months in a pessimistic scenario.
 
Lingering geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are likely to add further upward pressure through higher fuel costs, feeding into transportation prices in the coming months.
 An escalation or a durable ceasefire is the single highest-variance input to the inflation trajectory and Fed optionality.

3. **New Fed Chair Warsh's First Communications / FOMC Policy Signals** — 
Warsh assumed the Chair role on May 15; his first communications will be parsed carefully for whether he frames April's inflation data as an energy outlier (dovish read) or a services breakout (hawkish read).
 Any pivot in the Fed's reaction function under new leadership — particularly on the inflation tolerance threshold — is the key institutional risk for the rate/equity complex over the next 1–6 months. Watch the June FOMC (June 17–18) and the updated SEP dot plot as the definitive signal.

---
*All data as of market close May 22, 2026. Forward-looking statements are analytical, not investment advice.*

Tesla's Q1 2026 delivery numbers missed expectations, with 358,023 vehicles delivered — a 6% YoY increase but a 14.4% sequential decline — and a 50,000-vehicle inventory build-up, indicating a structural demand issue.



When a company that used to build-to-order is now sitting on five-figure excess inventory quarter after quarter, the demand problem is structural, not seasonal. Energy storage fell to 8.8 GWh — missing consensus by nearly 40% — removing the one pillar Tesla bulls had been leaning on.

### ☢️ TAIL RISK #1: U.S. Fiscal Sovereign Stress / Credit Rating Downgrade
**Probability Tier: ELEVATED**


As of May 2026, U.S. national debt has surged to nearly $39 trillion.
 
The deficit totals $1.9 trillion in fiscal year 2026 and is 5.8% of GDP — and is projected to grow to $3.1 trillion by 2036.



Fitch Ratings has warned that the U.S. credit rating risks slipping further. Fitch currently maintains the U.S. at AA+, having already downgraded from AAA in 2023 due to political run-ins over the debt ceiling.
 
"Structurally large fiscal deficits will keep the U.S.'s debt burden far above that of other 'AA' category sovereigns," Fitch analysts wrote.


**Trigger:** A Moody's downgrade to AA+/AA or a failed Treasury auction at a key maturity; 30Y yield breaking 5.5% on a sustained basis.

**What gets hurt most:**
- **Long-duration bonds (TLT):** Catastrophic duration risk in a yield-spike scenario
- **Rate-sensitive growth stocks:** NVDA, PLTR, SNOW — whose DCFs blow up at higher discount rates
- **Financials with Treasury holdings:** BAC, MS, GS — unrealized losses reprise March 2023 banking stress
- **USD:** Weakens, boosting non-USD assets like GLD and international equities


A waning investor confidence in timely resolution would likely be reflected in a steeper U.S. yield curve. Whether interest rates rise due to a larger credit-risk premium or fall given higher recession risk is less clear.
